mappings.rationale: Directly covers communicable disease, the multifactorial concept of contagious disease, endemic and epidemic disease, and disease control.
Covers the study of disease patterns in populations, including endemic vs epidemic disease and the prevention and control of disease.
Directly addresses epidemic disease spread, the multifactorial concept of contagious disease, and disease control measures.
Covers viruses as major agents of communicable disease in both animals and plants, and the mechanisms of viral pathogenesis.
Covers bacteria as agents of communicable disease, bacterial pathogenesis, and antibiotic resistance as a disease control challenge.
Directly covers immunity as a defense against disease, including immune response mechanisms and their role in disease prevention.
A specific example of communicable disease covering pathogenesis, epidemic spread, immunity issues, and disease control strategies.
Covers a major historical communicable disease illustrating endemic and epidemic disease patterns, and disease control efforts.
Addresses noncommunicable disease as a departure from homeostasis, including metabolic defects and environmental hazards.
Covers disease prevention and control at the population level, directly relevant to the control of communicable disease.
Covers drugs directed against disease organisms including antibiotics and chemotherapeutic drugs used in disease treatment.
Covers fungal plant and animal diseases, relevant to understanding both plant and animal disease agents.
Covers disease-causing microorganisms and their role in communicable disease across plants and animals.
Covers animal diseases including communicable diseases in non-human animals, their diagnosis, and treatment.
